name: powerlifted-htg-grounder-work
gate: relaxed: every baseline-solved pair still solves with plan cost <= baseline; trajectory not gated
note: deterministic grounder_atoms is a SCREEN, not a gate: 'cheaper-per-fact' wins leave it flat while cutting time. Base = master 6a46eb8 (incl. merged search-time SBO/grounder-overhead opts).

description: baseline: master 6a46eb8 (incl. merged search-time SBO/grounder-overhead opts) + grounder meter; 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh in a quiet window (loadavg ~1.1), gate passed all 5. NOTE ~28s not ~51s: the merged opts nearly halve wall-clock on this suite vs the stale base, while grounder_atoms is unchanged (47,644,479 / pushes 22,398,404 identical every sweep, 53% wasted productions -> redundant-work lever intact).

Exp1 (cheaper, counter FLAT): build join/project achiever bodies directly in the inline small_vector<int,2> via new Achievers(int,int,int,int)/Achievers(int,int,int) ctors, removing the per-produced-fact heap-allocated temporary std::vector<int> (join 2-int + project 1-int). grounder_atoms identical (47,644,479), so behavior-preserving. A/B was load-confounded on first try: cand window had rising load 0.59->1.41 -> DISCARD (cand 31.165 vs HEAD 30.728). Re-measured with a HEAD sandwich (HEAD before 30.728 / after 31.098, both quiet windows) around cand 29.622 -> KEEP. Pooled HEAD(10) median 31.012 vs cand(5) 29.622 = +4.48%, conf 2.20x, noise floor 2.47%. checks 62/62.

Exp2 (cheaper, counter FLAT): ReachedFacts::get_costs() returned std::vector<int> by value; changed to const-ref so product()'s min-cost scan stops copying the whole costs vector per condition per call. Removes a real per-product-call heap copy but only on the narrow product path (genome). A/B vs HEAD 79ee9c0: cand median 29.375 vs 29.506, +0.44% -> DISCARD (conf 0.17x, noise floor 4.2%). Sound but too small to clear suite noise. Reverted.

Exp3 (cheaper, counter FLAT): rewrote is_cheapest_path_to_achieve_fact to use phmap lazy_emplace, fusing the find()+insert() double hash/probe into one find_or_prepare_insert per NEW fact (~22.4M), and replacing the strictly-cheaper branch's erase+insert with an in-place const_cast set_cost (cost isn't hashed/compared; reached_facts achievers are never read back -- backchain reads achievers from Datalog::facts). Behavior-preserving: plan costs identical on heavy pairs d-13-2(79) and d-7-8(31), grounder_atoms flat. But the saved find-probe on small cache-hot genome args is too cheap: order-balanced interleaved full-suite A/B (9 rounds, alternating which binary runs first to cancel drift+position bias) gave paired C-H median -0.22s (~0.7%), mean -0.15s, with per-round noise +/-1-2s -> indistinguishable from zero. Naive 5v5 and per-pair screens were inconclusive/noisy. DISCARD. Reverted. NOTE methodology: always-H-first interleaving biased the 2nd-position sweep (thermal/cache); order-balancing is required on this box.

Exp4 (cheaper attempt -> REGRESSION): make reached_facts (flat_hash_set<Fact>) and newfacts reused members clear()'d per ground() call instead of local per-call allocation, to avoid re-malloc + growth-rehash every state eval (same pattern as JoinHashTable::clear). Counter flat, plan cost 79 preserved, peak_mem 65->60. But SLOWER: order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds, quiet window, noise floor 0.44%) gave C-H = +0.32s (1.16% slower) EVERY round, conf 3.24x. Reason: clear() is O(capacity); carrying a bucket array sized to the largest eval penalizes the many small evals (cold/sparse table) more than a fresh right-sized set. DISCARD. Reverted.

Exp5 (cheaper attempt -> REGRESSION): in join(), hoist the partner-side (head_pos, partner_arg_pos) mapping out of the per-partner loop (precompute once per call in a reused buffer) instead of re-calling get_head_position_of_arg + is_object for every matched partner. Counter flat, plan cost 79 preserved. But SLOWER: order-balanced A/B (7 rounds, noise floor 0.32%) C-H = +0.19s (0.68% slower) EVERY round, conf 2.02x. Reason: position_of is a cache-hot <=4-elem scan (already cheap), and the precompute pass now runs UNCONDITIONALLY including for the many zero-partner join calls where the original did zero inner work. DISCARD. Reverted.

Exp6 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +3.69%: product()'s nullary/ground-head path recomputed the cheapest reached tuple per condition by re-scanning ALL reached tuples on EVERY product() call (O(n^2) over the grounding) AND get_costs() returned the whole cost vector by value (a heap copy per scan). Now ReachedFacts tracks (min_cost, min_fact_index) incrementally in push_back (strict '<' preserves the old first-wins tie-break), so the ground-head branch is O(#conditions); also split the all-conditions-nonempty check into its own pass and skip the min work + nullary_head_achievers alloc entirely for non-ground heads (min-scan is unused there). Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs preserved d-13-2 gbfs-ff=79 / d-7-8 gbfs-add=31, checks 62/62. Measured via order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds, quiet window): C-H = -1.0s EVERY round, median 26.308 vs 27.315, conf 19.98x, noise floor 0.75%. Profile motivation: perf annotate showed product's hottest lines were the min-scan compare (weighted_grounder.cc:285-286) + get_costs copy. Committed 15b6379.

Exp7 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +4.39%: JoinHashTable::get_entries() looked up join partners with operator[], which INSERTS a spurious empty JoinHashEntry on a miss -- copying the vector<int> join key into the map and possibly rehashing. Zero-partner lookups are very common (a popped fact whose key has no partner yet), so this polluted the map on the hottest path. Switched the lookup to find() returning a static empty entry on miss (absent key yields the same empty range; insert side still uses operator[] correctly). Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs d-13-2 ff=79 / d-7-8 add=31, checks 62/62, peak_mem 65->61. Profile motivation: join was #1 at 16.84% self-time, all hot lines inside phmap SIMD probing (insert_fact_in_hash + get_facts_matching_key). Order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds): C-H = -1.16s EVERY round, median 25.250 vs 26.408, conf 14.46x, noise floor 0.70%.

Exp8 (REJECTED by counter, not timed): replace JoinHashEntry's flat_hash_set<Fact> with a plain std::vector<Fact> to skip hashing every fact on insert_fact_in_hash (join's hottest insert). Hypothesis 'each fact is inserted into an entry at most once' was WRONG: in this weighted (h^add/h^max) fixpoint a fact FIRES MULTIPLE TIMES at strictly-decreasing costs (a decrease-key re-push fires again to propagate the improved cost). The set's insert() is first-wins dedup, so an entry keeps each partner at its FIRST-fired (higher) cost and ignores later cheaper re-inserts -- that dedup is load-bearing. A vector accumulates the same partner at multiple costs -> duplicate head productions. Plan costs still 79/31 but grounder_atoms rose 47,644,479 -> 48,077,821 (+433,342) and pushes +217,120: a behavior change (more work + shifted achievers), violating the invariant. DISCARD on the deterministic counter alone, before timing. Reverted. LESSON: facts re-fire at decreasing costs; JoinHashEntry set-dedup semantics (first-fired wins) must be preserved.

Exp9=re-test of run-4's lazy_emplace, now KEEP +1.16%. Rewrote is_cheapest_path_to_achieve_fact with phmap lazy_emplace: one find_or_prepare_insert probe per produced fact instead of find()+insert() (two full arg-tuple hashes) on the NEW-fact path (~22.4M), plus in-place const_cast set_cost on the strictly-cheaper branch instead of erase+reinsert (cost isn't hashed/compared; reached_facts achievers are never read back -- backchain reads them from Datalog::facts). Identical to run 4's code; run 4 DISCARDED it because the A/B was in a noisy window (+-1-2s, ~0.7% signal lost). This time measured with the clean order-balanced interleaved protocol (7 rounds, HEAD noise floor 0.19%): C-H median -0.237s, C below HEAD in 6/7 rounds (round 6 a lone C-side blip), median 25.019 vs 25.313, +1.16%, conf 4.05x. Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs 79/31, checks 62/62. LESSON: measurement window quality, not the idea, decided run 4 vs run 9 -- sub-1.5% wins need the clean protocol. Committed 959a242.

Exp10 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +6.86% (biggest single win): changed JoinHashKey from std::vector<int> to utils::small_vector<int,2> (+ a JoinHashKeyHash functor using hash_range; join_key_buffer matched). Join keys hold one int per joining variable -- almost always 1-2 -- so an inline SBO key removes (a) a heap allocation per distinct key stored in the two join hash maps and (b) the pointer indirection when hashing/comparing keys, which happens on every join hash-table access (join was the #1 hot function at ~17.6% self-time). Content-based hash + element-wise operator== are identical to vector<int>, so the maps behave the same. Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs 79/31, checks 62/62, peak_mem 61->58. Order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds, noise floor 0.40%): C-H = -1.7s EVERY round, median 23.484 vs 25.215, +6.86%, conf 16.22x. Committed ffc9f68.

Exp11 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +0.85%: the main grounder loop copied the popped fact by value ('const Fact current_fact = datalog.get_fact_by_index(idx)') every pop, cloning its Arguments (small_vector<Term,4>) + achiever body. Changed to a reference, re-fetched by index (O(1)) inside the rule loop. The copy was defensive against fact-vector reallocation in is_cheapest_path; safe as a reference because project/join/product never insert into datalog.facts during a single call (inserts happen only in the is_cheapest loop after), and re-fetching per rule application picks up any realloc. Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs 79/31, checks 62/62. Small + near noise so measured carefully: first 7-round order-balanced A/B KEEP 2.07x/+0.84%; a pooled rerun went RERUN only due to a lone H=19.435 transient (CV 5.2%); a fresh clean 8-round A/B confirmed KEEP 2.49x/+0.86% (noise floor 0.49%), 14/16 rounds faster across both clean batches. Committed 0d2a2e8.

Exp12 (cheaper, counter FLAT) DISCARD ~0.4% (noise): apply the run-2 pattern to product()'s ground-head branch -- build the Achievers straight into its inline small_vector (default ctor + set_rule_index/cost + push_back per condition) instead of materializing a temporary std::vector<int> nullary_head_achievers and copying it in. Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat, plan costs 79/31, peak_mem 58->57. But narrow (only ground/nullary-head product calls, unlike run 2's broad join+project coverage): first 8-round order-balanced A/B looked like KEEP 2.04x/+1.0%, but pooling 12+12 clean rounds (excluding a round-1 H=20.665 fresh-invocation transient) collapsed to +0.40%, conf 0.56x -> DISCARD. LESSON: borderline ~2.0x confidence on 8 rounds can be optimistic; firm up before committing. Reverted.

Exp13 (cheaper, counter FLAT) DISCARD ~0.47% (noise): cache RuleType as an int in RuleMatcher::Match (set once at matcher construction) so the grounder's hot dispatch loop uses m.get_type() instead of the virtual rule.get_type() (called up to 2x per matched rule per pop). Behavior-preserving (grounder_atoms flat, costs 79/31). Order-balanced A/B, pooled 14+14 clean rounds: +0.47%, conf 0.99x -> DISCARD. Rule objects are cache-hot and the branch predictor handles the virtual call; no measurable gain. Reverted.

Exp14 (bundle) DISCARD ~0.3% (noise): bundled the two individually-sub-noise counter-flat micro-opts -- get_type cache (Exp13) + product ground-head Achievers-inline (Exp12) -- to test whether real-but-tiny wins stack past the noise floor. They do NOT: 10-round order-balanced A/B gave median +0.29%, conf 0.49x, diffs split ~5 neg/4 pos (excl. a round-10 H=24.707 transient). Confirms both micro-opts are at/below noise and their earlier ~0.5% single-batch KEEPs were noise-inflated. LESSON: the grounder is now optimized to where virtual-call elimination and narrow alloc removal give no measurable suite gain; only broad-hot-path or algorithmic (O(n^2)->O(n)) changes have landed. Reverted both.

Exp15 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +1.01%: replaced the per-evaluation phmap::flat_hash_set<int> initial_facts with a single int num_initial_facts. EDB+state facts are the first facts created each grounding (next_fact_index resets to 0, they're assigned before any rule fires), so they own the contiguous index range [0, num_initial_facts); membership is index < num_initial_facts. Removes: the per-eval set population (one insert per EDB+state fact), the set's memory, and turns backchain_from_goal's 3 .count() hash lookups into int comparisons. Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs 79/31, checks 62/62. Order-balanced A/B, pooled 13+13 clean rounds (excluded a round with a dual H=30.9/C=26.7 load-spike): ALL 13 rounds C faster, median 22.776 vs 23.009, +1.01%, conf 2.11x, noise floor 0.80%. Uniform direction (13/13) distinguishes this from the sub-noise micro-opts (runs 12-14). Committed 7ec9378.

Exp16 (cheaper, counter FLAT) DISCARD ~0.3% (noise): skip copying each fact's Arguments into ProductRule's per-condition ReachedFacts when the rule head is ground/nullary (add_reached_fact_to_condition passes store_args=!head_is_ground(); empty() switched to fact_indices since facts may be skipped). For ground heads product() only uses the incremental min (min_cost/min_fact_index), never the stored arg tuples, so the copy is wasted. Behavior-preserving (grounder_atoms flat, costs 79/31). But small: 8-round order-balanced A/B gave median +0.09% (conf 0.16x; clean ~0.3% excluding a round-6 C=24.652 spike) -> DISCARD. The small_vector<Term,4> arg copy is inline+cheap and ground-head product firing is a small fraction of grounder work. Reverted. Product micro-opts now all sub-noise post runs 6+7.

note: Contemporaneous order-balanced A/B (6 rounds, quiet window) of the loop's starting baseline (2e55861, run 1) vs current HEAD after 7 kept wins (runs 2,7,8,10,11,12,16). ~20% cumulative, every round 19.75-20.61% -- all wins stack cleanly, gain is real (not box drift).

description: Exp17 (REJECTED by counter + time regression): replace JoinHashEntry's flat_hash_set<Fact> with std::vector<Fact> + linear FIRST-WINS dedup. First instrumented join-entry sizes (throwaway, reverted): entries are TINY -- 28% empty, 72% size 1, <1% larger, max<=16, avg fan-out 0.76 -- so linear dedup has no O(n^2) risk and a per-entry hash set is almost pure overhead. BUT the counter moved to EXACTLY run 9's value (48,077,821) AND time regressed to ~29s (from ~22.8). KEY INSIGHT (explains run 9 definitively): the dedup was NOT the issue -- my linear dedup is correct. A flat_hash_set iterates partners in HASH order, a vector in INSERTION order; that different partner-iteration order changes which achiever each join head records and the whole production trajectory (h^add value is order-independent so plan costs stay 79/31 and the gate passes, but grounder_atoms and h^ff achievers move). This violates the hard invariant (reordering productions must not change recorded achievers) and regressed time. CONCLUSION: the flat_hash_set's iteration order is load-bearing; JoinHashEntry cannot be swapped for a vector. Lever CLOSED. Reverted (incl. instrumentation).
